sin-code --help
cmp stdout help.golden

-- help.golden --
sin-code is the unified Go binary for the SIN-Code tool suite.
It consolidates 19 subcommands (13 core tools + 6 utility commands) into a single cobra-based CLI:

  Core analysis:    discover, execute, map, grasp, scout, harvest, orchestrate
  Advanced tools:   ibd, poc, sckg, adw, oracle, efm
  Utility commands: security, sbom, config, self-update, tui, serve

Each subcommand is also a thin pass-through to the standalone tool repos
for backwards compatibility — the standalone binaries are still maintained
but "sin-code" is now the primary distribution channel.

Usage:
  sin-code [command]

Available Commands:
  adw         Architectural Debt Watchdogs — detect god modules, circular deps, etc.
  completion  Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
  config      View and manage sin-code configuration
  discover    Discover files with relevance scoring and pattern matching
  efm         Ephemeral Full-Stack Mocking — spin up disposable test environments
  execute     Execute shell commands safely with secret redaction and timeout
  grasp       Deep code understanding for a single file
  harvest     Fetch URLs with caching, structure extraction, and change detection
  help        Help about any command
  ibd         Intent-Based Diffing — compare code changes against stated intent
  map         Map code architecture with dependency graphs and hot-path analysis
  oracle      Verification Oracle — verify claims with evidence
  orchestrate Manage tasks with dependencies, parallel execution, and rollback plans
  poc         Proof-of-Correctness — verify code satisfies its specification
  sbom        Generate SPDX or CycloneDX JSON SBOM for a project
  sckg        Semantic Codebase Knowledge Graphs — build & query code graph
  scout       Search code with regex, semantic, symbol, and usage search
  security    Fast security analysis — auto-detects project type and runs available tools
  self-update Check for and install the latest sin-code release
  serve       Start an MCP server exposing all 13 sin-code tools
  tui         Interactive TUI menu for all sin-code subcommands

Flags:
  -h, --help      help for sin-code
  -v, --version   version for sin-code

Use "sin-code [command] --help" for more information about a command.
